title: 5-Stage CPU Pipeline Execution
description: A visual timeline showing the overlapping execution of five instructions (LD, ADD, SUB, ST, OR) as they progress through the five stages of a classic RISC pipeline (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB). The diagram demonstrates how pipelining increases throughput by allowing multiple instructions to be in different stages of processing simultaneously.
relationships: Shows the sequential flow of each instruction through the pipeline stages
Demonstrates the overlapping of stages between consecutive instructions
Highlights potential pipeline hazards (e.g., data dependency between ADD and SUB)
